steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states that only the Britannia (120 cannons, three decks) was ranked first-rate, while the Boyne (98 cannons, three decks) was not. First-rate ships had 850–875 crew; lower-ranked ships had fewer. The blank must state something that can be logically concluded from those facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'some ships with three decks had a crew of fewer than 850 people': The Boyne had three decks and was lower-ranked than first-rate, so it had fewer than 850 crew. That proves at least one three-deck ship had fewer than 850 crew. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the Britannia needed a crew larger than 875 people in order to operate efficiently': The passage only says first-rate ships had between 850 and 875 crew. Nothing supports exceeding 875. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the Boyne had a larger crew than the Britannia': The Boyne was lower-ranked, so it had fewer crew than the first-rate Britannia — the opposite of this claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'all ships with at least 98 cannons had a crew of at least 850 people': The Boyne had 98 cannons but fewer than 850 crew, directly contradicting this claim. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
